findings.title: JSON route conversion can accept unknown enum names when ordinals are missing
findings.suggested_fix: Treat JSON as unknown at the boundary and validate it before producing Route: require route.version === SCHEMA_VERSION, require every leg venue and amountMode to be own keys of VenueFamily/AmountMode, require the ordinal fields to be numbers that match those keys, and validate address/hex/string-bigint fields. Add negative tests for an unknown venue or amountMode with the ordinal omitted, because `VenueFamily[badName]` and the missing ordinal are both undefined today.

findings.title: Deadline patching hides payload ABI drift behind a broad cast
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the `abi as typeof solidlyPayloadAbi` escape hatch. Either export the concrete payload ABIs from each adapter and use small venue-specific patch helpers, or add a typed deadline-payload helper that preserves the ABI generic instead of pretending every payload is Solidly-shaped. Cover Solidly, UniswapV2, Slipstream, and Aerodrome deadline rewrites in materialization tests so schema drift fails close to the changed adapter.

residual_risks: The review was limited to static inspection plus `bun run --cwd router-ts typecheck`; runtime route execution and generated artifact parity tests were not run because the task requested read-only command use.

Production materialization still depends on callers supplying a correct deployed router address, recipient, deadline, and quote-derived minimums; those values are TypeScript-typed but not fully runtime-validated.
testing_gaps: No materializeRoute negative test covers schema-drifted JSON with an unknown venue or amountMode and missing ordinal fields.
No materializeRoute tests exercise the Solidly, UniswapV2, or Slipstream deadline patch branches.
No test asserts production materialization rejects zero or malformed routerAddress and recipient before constructing Universal Router payloads.
